channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Rescue operations underway after nine-storey building collapses in Angeles City, Philippines
item.description: A nine-storey building under construction collapsed before dawn on Sunday in Angeles City, Pampanga province, north of Manila, during a thunderstorm, prompting a major rescue operation. According to P...

item.title: UK medical tribunal removes Indian-origin oncologist from medical register following criminal conviction
item.description: An Indian-origin cancer specialist working with the National Health Service (NHS) in north-west England has been struck off the United Kingdom’s medical register following a tribunal decision linked t...

item.title: Kiambu Family Demands Justice After Polytechnic Student Dies in Police Custody
item.description: The family of 23-year-old architecture student Brian Njung'e Ndung'u is seeking answers after he was found dead while in custody at Kiambu Police Station shortly after his arrest over alleged burglary...

item.title: Man dies following shark attack near Tully Heads in Far North Queensland
item.description: A 39-year-old man has died after suffering critical injuries in a shark attack in Far North Queensland. The incident occurred near Tully Heads, about 120 kilometres south of Cairns, shortly after midd...

item.title: Telangana reports 16 suspected heatstroke deaths amid severe heatwave; government announces ₹4 lakh compensation
item.description: At least 16 people have died across several districts in Telangana due to suspected heatstroke amid an intense and prolonged heatwave, according to officials. The deaths have been reported from seven ...

item.title: Vancouver protest raises concerns about water and energy use tied to proposed AI data centres
item.description: Hundreds of protesters gathered in Vancouver to oppose two proposed artificial intelligence data centres planned by Telus, citing concerns about water consumption, electricity demand, and land use. Th...

item.title: 26 rescued after nine-storey building under construction collapses in Angeles City, Pampanga
item.description: At least 26 individuals were rescued after a nine-storey hotel under construction collapsed in Barangay Balibago, Angeles City, Pampanga early Sunday, May 24, 2026. Initial reports from authorities in...

item.title: APC Sources Claim Fubara Lost Party Backing Over Alleged Breach Of Political Agreement With Wike
item.description: Fresh reports from within the APC don show why Rivers State Governor, Siminalayi Fubara, allegedly lost support from President Bola Tinubu and the ruling party leadership. According to party insiders ...

item.title: UK nuclear site police report repeated internal security breaches involving lost sensitive materials
item.description: The article reports on ongoing internal security issues within the UK’s Civil Nuclear Constabulary (CNC), the armed police force responsible for protecting nuclear facilities and escorting nuclear mat...

item.title: Kenya Airways Flight Purser Dickson Nyibule Retires After Four Decades of Service
item.description: Kenya Airways veteran flight purser Dickson Nyibule has officially retired after serving the national carrier for 40 years. His final flight marked the conclusion of a long aviation career that starte...

item.title: Sotto says he proposed support for Gatchalian in Senate presidency discussions
item.description: Former Senate President Vicente Sotto III has addressed questions regarding his position on the ongoing discussions surrounding the Senate presidency. In the reported statement, Sotto clarified that r...

item.title: Sindh government, KMC and CAP launch Karachi Museum of History project at Clifton
item.description: The Sindh government, Karachi Metropolitan Corporation (KMC), and the Citizens Archive of Pakistan (CAP) have officially launched the Karachi Museum of History project with a groundbreaking ceremony h...

item.title: Senior doctor quits public health sector after exposing maladministration at Charlotte Maxeke Hospital
item.description: Prof Adam Mahomed, head of internal medicine at Charlotte Maxeke Johannesburg Academic Hospital, has announced that he is leaving the public health sector after years of frustration over conditions at...

item.title: Royal Mail first-class delivery performance falls short across UK postcode areas amid rising stamp costs
item.description: Official figures have revealed widespread delays in the UK’s first-class postal service, with Royal Mail failing to meet its delivery targets across all 124 postcode areas. The regulator Ofcom require...

item.title: US signals progress on negotiations with Iran involving the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: According to reporting from The Associated Press, former U.S. President Donald Trump has stated that a deal with Iran is largely negotiated, which would include provisions related to the opening of th...

item.title: Study Finds High-Altitude Red Auroras May Reveal Underestimated Geomagnetic Storms
item.description: A recent study conducted by researchers from Hokkaido University and the Okinawa Institute of Science and Technology has revealed that unusual red auroras observed over Japan may indicate that some ge...

item.title: Correctional officers’ union reports increase in violent incidents in Canadian prisons
item.description: A Canadian correctional officers’ union is warning that violent incidents inside federal prisons are becoming more frequent and more severe across the country. The union says correctional staff are in...

item.title: UK Audit Report Says Sizewell C Nuclear Power Could Cost Consumers More Than Hinkley Point
item.description: A report from the United Kingdom’s National Audit Office (NAO) has concluded that electricity generated by the planned Sizewell C nuclear power station in Suffolk is likely to cost consumers more than...

item.title: US Secret Service kills armed suspect after shooting incident near White House
item.description: A serious security incident took place near the White House in Washington DC when an armed man opened fire at a security checkpoint on Saturday evening. According to US Secret Service officials, the s...

item.title: Rise of the ‘gym jihad’ narrative in Hindutva political discourse
item.description: The article examines the emergence of the term “gym jihad” within sections of Hindutva discourse following recent police action in Uttar Pradesh. Authorities in Mirzapur invoked stringent laws, includ...
